rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs RAFIK EL-HANI and EL-HANI SERVICES INC.'s Motion To Amend Complaint. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to amend is granted. Plaintiffs have made the requisite showing that there was no undue delay in bringing the present motion, Defendant will not be prejudiced by the amendment, and that justice will be served by the amendment. (See CCP §473). rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 25, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff JANE DOE'S Motion To Compel Site Inspection, Camera Inspection Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear on both motions to discuss whether court has jurisdiction and if so, discuss merits of claim. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CATHY EILENBERGER UBELL et al VS. WILKES BASHFORD 375 SUTTER STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST ELEVATOR CORPORATION DBA AMTECH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Cross-Complainant Amtech Elevator Services' motion for summary adjudication as to the second and third causes of action for declaratory relief and express contractual indemnity is denied as a matter of law. In a noninsurance indemnity agreement, the language on point must be particularly clear and explicit, and will be construed strictly against the indemnitee. (See Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 553.) Construing the non-insurance contract against Amtech, the language in the agreement is not particularly clear and explicit to require Cross-Defendant Ed Mitchell West, LLC to indemnify and defend Amtech against third party claims arising from a breakdown or malfunction of the elevator. (See Declaration of Thiry in Support of Amtech's Motion for Summary Adjudication ("Thiry Decl."), Exh. A.) According to the plain language of the Agreement, Cross-Defendant's duty to indemnify Amtech is not embraced by the matters in Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint as it does not arise from personal injury or property damage caused by negligent acts or omissions during the performance of work. (See Civ. Code §2778(4); Thiry Decl., Exh. A.) Even if the two paragraphs in the Agreement were read in conjunction, the language should be interpreted to mean that Amtech intended to "not be bothered" by Ed Mitchell by claims arising during performance, and/or the malfunction of the elevator. (See Queen Villas Homeowners Association v. TCB Property Management (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1, 15.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALLEN GROSSMAN VS. JOHN ST. CROIX et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2013, Line 17, PETITIONER ALLEN GROSSMAN's Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Petitioner's verified petition for peremptory writ of mandate is granted. Respondents' request for judicial notice is granted. The record shows that Respondents have not met their burden that the withheld documents are exempt under the California Public Records Act and the San Francisco Sunshine Ordinances. (See Gov. Code §6255; SF Admin. Code §67.21(g).)) Under the San Francisco Sunshine Ordinance, public records regarding advice on compliance with, analysis of, and opinion concerning liability under, or any communication other wise concerning the CPRA or the Sunshine Ordinance are subject to disclosure. (San Francisco Admin. Code §67.24(b)(1)(iii).) Respondents concede that the 24 remaining responsive documents consist of requests from the Ethics Commission's staff to Respondents for legal advice concerning the Commission's proposed regulations for Sunshine complaints, and Respondents' memorandums in response that analyze the legal issues. (See Declaration of Shen in Support of Respondents' Opposition to Petition for Writ of Mandate, 7.) Respondents are ordered to deliver to Petitioner copies of the remaining responsive documents requested by Petitioner in his October 3, 2012 request. Petitioner's request for costs and attorney fees under Gov. Code § 6259(d) is denied without prejudice. Petitioner has not submitted a supporting declaration of costs and fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ERICA SKONE-REES VS. WEST ELM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANTs WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. and WEST ELM DBA WILLIAMS-SONOMA STORES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff. Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Within 10 days, Plaintiff shall provide verified responses to the Requests for Admission, and Plaintiff is permitted to raise her 5th Amendment right to not incriminate herself. To the extent that the definition of "injury" is at issue, in each of these responses, Plaintiff shall identify the injury or injuries and respond substantively to each request. Defendant has not sufficiently demonstrated that this action requires the excessive number of special interrogatories propounded, and it is therefore denied. (CCP § § 2030.030(a)(1), 2030.050.) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD JOHN RILEY VS. ULRIC E.J. USHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The tort claims are barred by the litigation privilege, Cal. Civ. Code §47(b). The Complaint states that it was represented to Wells Fargo in the Notice of Levy and Writ of Execution "that Plaintiff account holder was obliged to the judgment." (First Amended Complaint, 27.) Claims "based on the instructions given to the bank by a Sheriff's Department employee [are] barred by the litigation privilege." (Tom Jones Enterprises, Ltd. v. County of Los Angeles (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 1283, 1294.) As to the fifth cause of action, the contract attached as Exhibit A to the FAC and extensively quoted in the FAC explicitly states that it is "Effective April 1, 2013." The alleged breach occurred on May 29, 2012, and therefore, the attached and quoted contract could not have been the operative agreement at the time of the alleged breach. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend, if he can in good faith, facts relating to the operative contract at the time of the alleged breach. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD JOHN RILEY VS. ULRIC E.J. USHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTs ULRIC USHER and PATRICIA HAMILTON's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. The Court finds that the gravamen of Plaintiff's complaint arises out of the wrongful withholding of money by Defendants, and therefore does not arise from a protected activity. (Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169, 1190, "for anti-SLAPP purposes this gravamen is defined by the acts on which liability is based, not some philosophical thrust or legal essence of the cause of action.") Plaintiff's evidence establishes that Plaintiff notified Defendants multiple times that they levied on the wrong account and Defendants have wrongfully refused to return the funds. (See Complaint, 9; Decl. of Richard John Riley, 12-17.) Thus, Defendants did not make the requisite threshold showing under CCP §425.16 that Plaintiff's causes of action arise out of an act in furtherance of a person's right to petition or free speech, and must be denied. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees and costs in the amount of $5,500 is granted. Plaintiff has established that this motion was frivolous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
